name: XERIANT, INC.
business: Xeriant, Inc. is dedicated to the discovery, development and commercialization of disruptive technologies in advanced materials and aerospace. Their advanced materials line includes NEXBOARD, an eco-friendly, patent-pending composite construction panel made from plastic and fiber waste, designed to replace products such as drywall, plywood, OSB, MDF, MgO board and other materials used in construction. They seek to partner with and acquire strategic interests in visionary companies that accelerate this mission.

exposures.sentence: Xeriant s advanced materials line, when available, will be marketed under the DUREVER brand, and includes NEXBOARD , an eco-friendly, patent-pending composite construction panel made from plastic and fiber waste, designed to replace products such as drywall, plywood, OSB, MDF, MgO board and other materials used in construction.

The Company has developed a nanotechnology-enhanced composite construction panel, called NEXBOARD, made from plastic and fiber waste to replace products such as drywall, plywood, OSB, MDF, MgO board and other materials used in construction.
The Company owns a 64% interest in its subsidiary, American Aviation Technologies, LLC ( AAT ), which owns a patented VTOL drone/aircraft concept called Halo.

tamClaims.claim: The smart nano-construction materials market, valued at USD 12.5 billion in 2023, is projected to reach USD 17.7 18.7 billion by 2032 (CAGR 4.5 4.9%).
tamClaims.sentence: The smart nano-construction materials market, valued at USD 12.5 billion in 2023, is projected to reach USD 17.7 18.7 billion by 2032 (CAGR 4.5 4.9%), driven by demand for high-performance, eco-friendly materials.

tamClaims.claim: The global aerospace materials market, valued at USD 43.23 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 74.29 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 7%.
tamClaims.sentence: The global aerospace materials market, valued at USD 43.23 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 74.29 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 7% (Fortune Business Insights).

tamClaims.claim: The green construction materials market is projected to reach USD 1.8 trillion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: NEXBOARD is initially poised to capitalize on the burgeoning green construction materials market, projected to reach USD 1.8 trillion by 2030, per a 2024 World Economic Forum report.
